zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Keith於1320年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾可利福時，她的父親John Keith Marischal of Scotland26歲，母親Isabella de Soulis44歲。她於1339年與David de la Haye 6th Lord of Errol在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1410年在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾享年90歲。

nameMeanings.name: Keith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the lands of Keith in East Lothian. The placename is derived from British Celtic cait- ‘wood’. In the 17th century numerous bearers of this name settled in Ulster.
German: nickname from Middle High German kīt ‘sprout, offspring’.

History: George Keith (c. 1638–1716), born at Peterhead, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, came to NJ in or before 1685. In 1689 he settled in Philadelphia, where he became headmaster of the school now called the William Penn Charter School. He came into sharp collision with the Quaker leaders in PA and formed a separatist party known as the Christian Quakers, popularly known as ‘Keithians’.

variants.name: Kight
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Kite  .

variants.name: Keath
meanings: Scottish and English (mainly Cambridgeshire): variant of   Keith  .

variants.name: Kite
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English kete, kyte ‘kite’ (the bird of prey), perhaps a nickname for a rapacious person.
English: habitational name from one or more of the minor places with names deriving from Old English cȳte, cyte ‘cottage, hut, shed’, such as Kite Farm in Swalecliffe (Kent).

variants.name: Keeth
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Keith  .

variants.name: Keitt
meanings: Possibly an altered form of German   Keith   2.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Keith was born in 1320, in Crieff, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, John Keith Marischal of Scotland, was 26 and her mother, Isabella de Soulis, was 44. She married David de la Haye 6th Lord of Errol in 1339, in Midlothian, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1410, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 90.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Keith(이)가 1320년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 크리프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Keith Marischal of Scotland(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella de Soulis(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 1339년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언에서 David de la Haye 6th Lord of Errol(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1410년 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Keith родилась в 1320 г. в местности криефф, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Keith Marischal of Scotland было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Soulis, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David de la Haye 6th Lord of Errol в 1339 г. в местности мидлотян, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1410 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 90 лет.
